title: Topical nerve stimulator and sensor for sexual function
abstract: What is provided is a method and apparatus to modify sexual function by selectively electrically stimulating a mammalian nerve comprising: applying a dermal patch having an integral electrode in proximity to a sensory nerve associated with a penis or clitoris; determining one or more stimulations corresponding to the sensory nerve, by logic of the dermal patch; applying the stimulation by the electrodes and a stimulator integral to the dermal patch to produce an electric field; and selectively activating the sensory nerve by the electric field to produce an action potential in the sensory nerve that travels to a sacral segment of a spinal cord resulting in a reflex that modifies sexual function.
claims:
Claims M-0003 Draft Claims v4 1. A method of modifying sexual function by selectively electrically stimulating a mammalian nerve comprising: applying a dermal patch having an integral electrode in proximity to a sensory ' nerve associated with a penis or clitoris; determining one or more stimulations corresponding to the sensory nerve, by logic of the dermal patch ; applying the stimulation by the electrodes and a stimulator integral to the dermal patch to produce an electric field; and selectively activating the sensoiy nerve by the electric field to produce an action potential in the sensory nerve that travels to a sacral segment of a spinal cord resulting in a reflex that modifies sexual function, 2. The method of claim I, further comprising the stimulation producing an action potential in the sensory nerve that travels up the spmal cord to a brain producing sexual arousal; the sexual arousal producing an action potential traveling down the spinal cord further modifying sexual function. 3. The method of claim 1 further comprising the stimulation producing a spinal reflex that sends an action potential to the penis to produce an erection. 4. The method of claim 1 , further comprising the stimulation producing a spinal refle that sends a action potential to a muscle in the penis, or a seminal vesicle or one or more other parts of the pelvis, or one or more secretory organs causing ejaculation. 5. The method of claim 1 , further comprising the stimulation of the sensory nerve in the penis or clitoris creating an action potential traveling up the spinal cord to a brain and down to one or more muscles or organs in the pelvis, hips, breasts, upper and lower limbs and other parts of the body; the action potential producing a complex sensation of orgasm. 6. The method of claim 1 , further comprising receiving a manual command from a remote control device to active the stimulation. 7. The method of claim 1 further comprising: 

 transmitting an acoustic pulse into a tissue in proximity to the sensory nerve by an acoustic transceiver integral on the dermal patch; receiving acoustic information to derive an image of a penis and determine when the penis is losing its erection; and activating the stimulation generator to maintain the erection based upon the received acoustic information. 8. The method of claim 1 further comprising transmitting image data received by an acoustic transceiver mtegral to the dermal patch to a remote control device for processing and image recognition. 9. The method of claim 1 further comprising recording an activation profile by the dermal patch and sending the activation profile from the dermal patch to a remote control device. 10. The method of claim 1 further comprising gathering data from more sensors on a continuous basis or only when the dermal patch is activated. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising using the sensor data to alter the stimulation. 12. An apparatus for modifying sexual function by selectively electrically stimulating a mammalian nerve comprising: a dermal patch; one or more stimulation generators integral to the patch; and logic to create a stimulation. 13. The apparatus of claim 12 further comprising: a senso to identify one or more activities or electrophysiological properties of a sensory nerve associated with a penis or clitoris, and logic to use the activities and properties identified to create or modify a stimulation for the selected sensory nerve. 14. The apparatus of claim 12 further comprising logic configured to accept a manual activation command from a remote control device. 15. The apparatus of claim 12 further comprising an acoustic transceiver to transmit an acoustic pulse into a tissue in proximity to the sensory nerve and receive acoustic information to derive an image of a penis and determine 

 when the penis is iosing its erection; and logic to activate the stimulation generator to maintain the erection based upon the received acoustic information. 16. A method of modifying sexual function by selectively mechanically stimulating a mammalian nerve comprising: applying a dermal patch having an integral mechanical stimulator in proximity to a sensory nerve associated with a penis or clitoris; determining one or more stimulations corresponding to the sensory nerve, by logic of the dermal patch; applying the stimulation by mechanical stimulator integral to the dermal patch to activate the nerve; and selectively producing an action potential in the sensory nerve that travels to a sacral segment of a spinal cord resulting in modification of sexual function. 17. The method of claims 16, further comprising the stimulation producing an action potential in the sensory nerve that travels up the spinal cord to a brain producing sexual arousal; the sexual arousal producing an 

 action potential traveling down the spinal cord to further modify sexual function. 18. The method of claim 16 further comprising the stimulation producing a spinal reflex that sends an action potential to the penis to produce an erection. 19. The method of claim 16, further comprising the stimulation producing a spinal reflex that sends a action potential to a muscle in the penis, or a seminal vesicle or one or more other parts of the pelvis, or one or more secretory organs causing ejaculation. 20. The method of claim 16, further comprising the stimulation of the sensory nerve in the penis or clitoris creating an action potential traveling up the spinal cord to a brain and down to one or more muscles or organs in the pelvis, hips, breasts, upper and lower limbs and other parts of the body; the action potential producing a complex sensation of orgasm.
